mission.title: cloudevents: A schema (constraints) for context and data attributes identified by schemaURL
mission.description: A schema (constraints) for context and data attributes identified by schemaURL . Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
Many of the context attributes (e.g., eventType, source, eventID, contentType) are not tightly constrained to maintain flexibility in specific implementations.

steps.title: Apply the fix for A schema (constraints) for context and data attributes…
steps.description:
when you say "schema" can you elaborate a bit on how receivers will use that info? I'm trying to mentally walk through how a receiver of an CE would process the incoming event:
- I would think the first thing they would look at would be either the `source` and/or `type` to determine if this event is of interest to them. `source` obviously would be of interest if they only want events from certain event producers.
- `type` is probably pretty close to the `schema` thing you're looking for. While

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "A schema (constraints) for context and data attributes…" is functioning correctly.
